pimpQuestions: Q: What approach is best used for ORIF of distal radius fractures? A: Volar approach is best for volarly displaced fractures (Smith and volar Barton) and has also become standard for dorsally displaced fractures (Colles) and some intra-articular fractures.
Q: What are the acceptable reduction parameters of distal radius fractures? A: Radial shortening <3mm, Dorsal tilt <10 degrees, Intraarticular step-off less than 2mm.
Q: What is the most common complication following ORIF of distal radius fractures? A: Rupture of the FPL tendon.
Q: What is the most commonly used surgical approach to the distal radius? A: Volar Henry and its modifications (FCR approach).
otherUsefulFacts: Restoration of radial bow is paramount in fixation of radial shaft fractures to prevent pronation and supination loss.

Acceptable post-reduction parameters for treating a distal radius fracture nonoperatively include: Radial height: <5 mm of shortening, Volar tilt: Dorsal angulation <5° or within 20° of contralateral side, Radial inclination: <5° difference to contralateral, Articular step-off: <2 mm.

selected.rationale: Volar wrist (distal Henry) approach is the standard approach for ORIF of distal radius fractures.

approachSelection.notes: Only one allowed approach in catalog matches distal radius ORIF anatomy and surgical practice.

questions.q: Which two major structures are typically encountered first in the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach?
In the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach, which nerve is primarily at risk including its recurrent motor and palmar cutaneous branches?
When performing the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach, what is the primary clinical indication related to the distal radius?
questions.answer: Distal radius open reduction and internal fixation (ORIF)

questions.q: Is the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach considered to utilize an internervous plane?
questions.answer: No, it is not an internervous plane.

questions.q: Which artery runs superficial and is encountered in the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach?
Name the two specific branches of the median nerve that are at risk in the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach.
questions.answer: Recurrent motor branch and palmar cutaneous branch

questions.q: In which anatomic region is the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach performed?
questions.answer: Wrist region of the upper extremity

questions.q: What surgical area related to nerve decompression can be accessed through the volar wrist (distal Henry) approach?
